title: Enterprise Fraud Management
body_text:
Enterprise Fraud Management
1	Introduction to Enterprise Fraud Management
1.1	Introduction to Enterprise Fraud Management
Central 1âs Enterprise Fraud Management (EFM) system provides alerts for potentially fraudulent transactions. EFM has strong functional fraud monitoring, detection, and prevention abilities, which include the use of fraud models, machine learning, artificial intelligence, and rules. Your financial institution uses the alerts generated in EFM to investigate suspected fraud and to report the results in EFM.
EFM is primarily used to monitor Interac e-Transfer
Â®
payments since Interac e-Transfer fraud is a major fraud risk resulting in significant fraud losses to financial institutions. EFM also supports fraud monitoring for bill payments, mobile deposits, and wires. Central 1 will share the availability of fraud monitoring for other payment types such as AFTs once plans are confirmed.
Central 1 provides a fraud report on the 15th of every month. If the 15th is a non-business day, the report is available on the following business day. You can download this report from Central 1âs File and Report Exchange. See the File Exchange and Report Distribution Part for information on accessing the File and Report Exchange.
